name: HMS ML 454 (ML 454)
events: 3 Jan 1943 With her refit completed, HrMs O 10 (Lt.Cdr. Baron D.T. Mackay, RNN), departed Blyth for Dundee. She was escorted by HMS ML 454 (T/Lt. W.M. Russell, RNVR). (1)

23 Mar 1943 HrMs O 14 (Lt.Cdr. H.A.W. Goossens, RNN) departed Dundee for trials in the North Sea to the East of Dundee. She was escorted by the British Motor Launch HMS ML 454 (T/Lt. W.M. Russell, RNVR). (2)

6 Jun 1943 HMS Untiring (Lt. R. Boyd, DSC, RN) departed Blyth for Holy Loch. She was initially escorted by HMS ML 300 (CO ?) until 1600 hours when HMS ML 454 (T/Lt. W.M. Russell, RNVR) took over the escort.
At 0635/7 HMS Stella Pegasi (T/Lt. T.G. Hornsby, RNR) took over the escort and finally at 1740/7 HMS Sabre (Lt. R.L. Caple, DSC, RN) took over the escort. (3)
ADM numbers indicate documents at the British National Archives at Kew, London.
